We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that the City Mayor:
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Assessor already requested the Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) for certifications if the owners/developers of the 8 subdivisions renewed their business permits so that appropriate action could be undertaken.
observations.reason: There are several titles, which have been delivered to the City by the owner or representativ e without proper Deed of Donation. Developers would want to donate these properties (road lots and open spaces) in favor of the City of Imus. However, they intentionally omitted payment of real property taxes, arguing that these properties are for the benefit of the public/consti tuents. City is barred from accepting these donations until the arrears in Real Property Taxes have been fully settled. The corporate personality of some transferors /developers ceased to exist, which is akin to abandonment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City officials concerned to observe strictly Section 70 of the MNGAS, Volume I and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the prompt submission of accounts and financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Reports of Collections and Deposits (RCD), Reports of Disbursements (RD) together with disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports were submitted.
observations.reason: Delayed Submission in the last quarter of 2016 due to voluminous transactions

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat and/or the City Engineering Office to ensure that copies of approved contracts and all documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation be furnished to the Audit Team within five days from their execution as prescribed under Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against the remiss officials.
observations.managementAction: Some copies of approved contracts and their supporting bidding and technical documents were submitted within five (5) working days after their perfection and/or approval.
observations.reason: Copies of notarized contracts were not returned to the GSO on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board, in the preparation of the Special Education Fund Annual Budget, be guided by the provisions of existing rules and regulations so that only the authorized priority expenditures identified in the governing rules be incorporated therein to ensure proper utilization of the fund. Otherwise, the Board should seek a resolution of the said issue from the DepEd and/or the oversight agencies which include the DBM and DILG.
observations.reason: Filing of Appeal for the Notice of Disallowance to COA Director/Commi ssion Proper is to be made by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City Mayor direct the General Services Officer to furnish the Auditor with copies of Purchase/Work Orders and delivery documents within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009, otherwise, file administrative charges against the latter.
observations.managementAction: The copies of Purchase Orders/Work Orders and supporting documents were submitted in bulk.
observations.reason: Copies of some Purchase Orders/Work Orders and supporting documents were not forward ed/returned to the GSO on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to ensure that all acquired lands (purchased and donated) are recorded in the books of accounts and tallied with the property records and related reports being kept by the General Services Officer and City Treasurer In addition, instruct the City General Services Officer and the City Assessor to see to it that titles of the 188 parcels of land are transferred in the name of the City Government of Imus to establish the City’s absolute ownership thereof.

We recommended  and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to account both Leave Benefits and Retirement Gratuity payables back to Equity account while the City Budget Officer ensure that budget for the said payable accounts are available and included in the Annual/ Supplemental Budget.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to identify the appropriate expense accounts to be used for the meals and snacks charged to Food Supplies Expense and make the necessary adjustments to correct erroneous entries made to Food Supplies Expense,  Other Bonuses and Allowances as well as the Telephone Expenses.  Henceforth, ensure that appropriate expense accounts are used to record and reflect in the Statement of Financial Performance the correct and actual expenses incurred as of year-end.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of the stale checks so that appropriate remedial actions can be undertaken to replace the said stale checks.  Likewise, require the City Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) by debiting the Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable to record the actual cash account balance as of year-end.  Henceforth, strictly observe the specific policies and procedures in accounting for stale checks.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDO) to explain the loss of the three tablets and identify the persons liable and/or responsible for the loss and to submit the application for relief from property accountability supported with complete and proper documents enumerated above.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the officers and employees of the City to exercise due diligence in the custodianship of government properties.  In case of loss of government property, require the accountable officer to file immediately the notice of loss and the request for relief from property accountability within the reglementary period of 30 days from the occurrence of loss, with the Auditor concerned.  If the request will not be granted, the loss shall be taken up as a receivable from the accountable officer.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City General Services Officer (GSO) and BAC Chairman to see to it that adequate monitoring on the expiration date of the contract of  services  for garbage collection, hauling and disposal is  strictly observed so that conduct of public bidding is promptly done to avoid extension of service contracts as much as possible.  Henceforth, extension of ongoing contracts for general support services that are essential, indispensable, or necessary to support the operations of the procuring entities must be communicated to the GPPB prior to the expiration of the six (6) months period.
City Environment and Natural Resource Officer (CENRO)  to  see to it that the actual volume collected  per trip are closely monitored to determine compliance with the Terms of Reference of the MOA/contract as to the total volume of garbage generated  and to support monthly payment for the garbage collection, hauling and disposal; and
City General Services Officer and CENRO to submit the details of the computations of the unit cost of P402.81 and P410.00 per cubic meter for Calendar Years 2015 and 2016.
We also recommended that the City Mayor ensure that contract for services shall be executed thru a Service Contract instead of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to submit a written justification for non-compliance with the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016 dated October 7, 2016.   Henceforth, comply strictly with the guidelines in the preparation of the ABC for all succeeding infrastructure projects of the City as prescribed by the DPWH and confirmed by the BIR.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documentations for evaluation and review of the Audit Team.  Henceforth, ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before payments to avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer to remit immediately the balance of the Due to Local Government Units (LGUs) totalling P47,507,398.18.  Henceforth, ensure that all succeeding shares of the Provincial Government and the 97 barangays of the City are promptly remitted.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to ensure that at least five (5) calendar days before the scheduled procurement activity/ process, the BAC should invite in writing, representatives from the Commission on Audit and from a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand, and from a non-government organization in accordance with the IRR of RA No. 9184.
We also recommended that the BAC Chairman ensure that conduct of the required Pre-procurement Conference for the procurement of goods with an ABC of more than Two Million Pesos (P2,000,000.00)  be strictly observed.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statements of all their gross sales/receipts (not only from the City Government of Imus) for the preceding calendar year and other pertinent records/ information for determination of gross receipts;
City Accountant to submit to the City Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and
City Treasurer to verify/assess the gross receipts of the six (6)  contractors/suppliers and validate the reported undercollection totalling P1,045,363.25, and enforce the collection of the underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the City’s Revenue Code.  Absence of documented action or information to counter the undercollection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.

We recommended that the City Mayor refund the amount of P234,015.54 representing excessive claims for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) and submit  to the Office of the Auditor all official receipts and other supporting documents necessary,  depending on the nature of expense charged for review and evaluation. Limit the annual appropriation and disbursements for discretionary purpose of the City Mayor within two (2) percent of the actual receipts derived from the basic property tax in the next preceding calendar year, ensure that claims are supported by receipts and other supporting documents necessary depending on the nature of expense charged, to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City officials concerned to observe strictly the prompt submission of accounts and financial reports as prescribed in the government provisions of the laws, rules and regulations cited therein, otherwise, it may be a ground for imposing administrative charges against the erring City officials.

We recommended that the City Mayor, City Vice Mayor and Sangguniang Panlungsod Members (SPMs) exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds and refrain from incurring irregular, unnecessary, excessive, or extravagant expenditures.  Submit to the COA Audit Team supporting documents for the subject meal expenses, particularly the purposes of the travels, (or visit) meetings attended, names of the guests and government agencies visited, the projects involved and justification that the expenditures are necessary in the performance of their duties and in the attainment of the objectives or thrusts of the City Government, for review and further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Health Officer and other requisitioning officers to ensure that every Requisition and Issue Voucher (RIV) or any request to purchase drug shall be accompanied by a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF, Volume I as required under Executive Order (EO) No. 49 dated January 21, 1993.

We recommended that the City Mayor require responsible officials or in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items, to comply strictly with the submission of delivery documents to the Auditor within twenty-four hours upon acceptance of such deliveries pursuant to Commission on Audit Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 in order that the Auditor can independently inspect and attest that deliveries were made within the terms and conditions set forth in the purchase contracts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Mayor direct the CDRRMO to:
Prepare the DRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format as required under Sections 5.1.1 and 5.1.2 of COA Circular  No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
Prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) duly certified by the City Accountant on or before the 15th day after each month through the DRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor.

We recommended that the City Mayor set deadlines for both the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to complete the reconciliation of their property records/balances and the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all the City’s PPE.  Trace the origin of the PPE accounts with differences to facilitate reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the General Services already conducted an actual physical count of five (5) PPE accounts with a total amount of P37,262,909.88, under the Special Education Fund (SEF).
observations.reason: No available documents to support prior years’ procurement/acquisitions

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to establish with certainty the reported RPT/SET Receivable as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The certified list of taxpayers and taxes due and collectible was already prepared and submitted to the City Accountant by the City Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City Mayor require the City officials and employees concerned to liquidate their outstanding cash advances of P43,200.00 in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004.
observations.managementAction: The total amount of P2,700.00 was already liquidated as of December 31, 2016.  The remaining balance of P40,500.00 was refunded on January 26, 2017 under Official Receipt No. CI 0628112.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct: the City Accountant to refrain from directly recording the procurement or receipt of supplies and materials to expense account, but instead adopt the Perpetual Inventory System of accounting using as basis the SSMI to be submitted by the General Services Office in recording the issuance of the said supplies and materials; and the General Services Officer to maintain supplies ledger cards for each item of supplies purchased/acquired.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer already maintained supplies ledger cards for each item of supplies and materials purchased/acquired starting the later part of 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require that the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF be prepared regularly, certified correct by the City Accountant and submitted by the CDRRM Officer to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF were prepared but not in the prescribed format.
observations.reason: The CDRRMO was not conversant with the required reporting format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned City officials to follow up and make appropriate representation with the National Solid Waste Management Commission for the expeditious approval of the City’s 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan to ensure the City’s compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) of the City of Imus was conditionally approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission under NSWMC resolution No. 692 series of 2016 on December 13, 2016 subject to the satisfactory submission of some additional requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor include in its Solid Waste and Management Plan any incentives/rewards in the grant of the same pursuant to Section 17(m) of RA No. 9003 and in accordance with the framework and objective of the same law.
observations.managementAction: The City of Imus already included in its Solid Waste and Management Plan incentives/reward s and the criteria/guidelin es in the grant of the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require that the guidelines in Rule VI, Section 37.c of RA No. 9710, otherwise known as the ‘Magna Carta of Women’, be strictly followed for the creation, strengthening and institutionalizati on of the City’s GFPS.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) was already in accordance with Rule VI, Section 37.c of RA No. 9710, otherwise known as the ‘Magna Carta of Women’ and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: limit the hiring of job order employees to what is prudent and necessary in the exigencies of service; direct the Human Resource Management Officer to evaluate the contribution or necessity of each Job Order employee to the Office’s performance before the renewal of contract; and direct the concerned City officials to comply with the prohibition under CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40, series of 1998, on hiring of retired or over-aged personnel as JOs.
observations.managementAction: The total number of hired Job Orders was reduced from 2,520 to 1,907 or 24.32%. The Human Resource Management Officer already evaluated the contribution or necessity of each Job Order employee to the Office’s performance before the renewal of contract; All retired or over-aged JO personnel were already terminated.
